channel.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories
channel.description: Top stories in the U.S. and world news, politics, health, science, business, music, arts and culture. Nonprofit journalism with a mission. This is NPR.
channel.copyright: Copyright 2024 NPR - For Personal Use Only

image.title: NPR Topics: Home Page Top Stories

item.title: Trump says he has 'no problem' with Russian oil tanker bringing relief to Cuba despite blockade
item.description: President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.
item.content:encoded:
President Donald Trump said he has "no problem" with a Russian oil tanker off the coast of Cuba delivering relief to the island, which has been brought to its knees by a U.S. oil blockade.

(Image credit: Ramon Espinosa)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: Central Haitian town descends into fire and bloodshed from gang warfare
item.description: Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.
item.content:encoded:
Violence erupted in the central Haitian town of Petite-Rivière de l'Artibonite early Sunday morning as a powerful gang warred with a vigilante group.

(Image credit: Odelyn Joseph)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: The Final Four is set with UConn stunning Duke to join Illinois, Arizona and Michigan
item.description: The Huskies beat Duke with a 3-pointer from the logo with 0.4 seconds left by Braylon Mullins, who grew up just outside of Indianapolis.
item.content:encoded:
The Huskies beat Duke with a 3-pointer from the logo with 0.4 seconds left by Braylon Mullins, who grew up just outside of Indianapolis.

(Image credit: Stephanie Scarbrough)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: ICE officers could remain at airports after TSA workers are paid
item.description: Even when Transportation Security Administration workers get paid, Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents could still be present at U.S. airports.
item.content:encoded:
Even when Transportation Security Administration workers get paid, Immigration and Customs Enforcement agents could still be present at U.S. airports.

(Image credit: David J. Phillip)
item.dc:creator: Jeff Brady

item.title: Pope Leo XIV rejects claims that God justifies war in Palm Sunday Mass message
item.description: Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.
item.content:encoded:
Pope Leo XIV rejected claims that God justifies war and prayed especially for Christians in the Middle East during a Palm Sunday Mass before tens of thousands of people in St. Peter's Square.

(Image credit: Remo Casilli)
item.dc:creator: The Associated Press

item.title: How to navigate the maze of drug discounts to get the best price
item.description: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.
item.content:encoded: In February, TrumpRx joined a growing list of websites consumers can tap for discounts on their medicines. Here's a cheat sheet for getting the best deal.
item.dc:creator: Sydney Lupkin

item.title: Sunday Puzzle: Vowel Renewals
item.description: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.content:encoded: NPR's Don Gonyea plays the puzzle with KXJZ radio listener Suzanne Palmer and Weekend Edition Puzzlemaster Will Shortz.
item.dc:creator: Will Shortz

item.title: Iran warns U.S. against ground invasion, as Pakistan holds diplomatic talks
item.description: A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.
item.content:encoded:
A high-ranking Iranian official has accused the U.S. of planning a ground invasion as part of the next stage in the Iran war, and said such an intervention would be met with force.

(Image credit: Vahid Salemi)
item.dc:creator: NPR Staff

item.title: Why a 98-year-old federal judge is asking the Supreme Court for her job back
item.description: Pauline Newman's story shines a light on the aging judiciary, where judges are getting older and lifetime tenure is raising thorny questions about retirement.
item.content:encoded:
Pauline Newman's story shines a light on the aging judiciary, where judges are getting older and lifetime tenure is raising thorny questions about retirement.

(Image credit: Paul J. Richards)
item.dc:creator: Carrie Johnson

item.title: He wants children's bikes made in the U.S.A. — and tariffs against his rivals
item.description: Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.
item.content:encoded:
Nearly all the bicycles sold in the United States are made overseas. An Indiana company set out to change that — and it's seeking a push from the Trump administration's tariffs.

(Image credit: Scott Horsley)
item.dc:creator: Scott Horsley
